title: The Sand-Man
content:
I know a man
    With face of tan,
    But who is ever kind;
    Whom girls and boys
    Leaves games and toys
    Each eventide to find.

    When day grows dim,
    They watch for him,
    He comes to place his claim;
    He wears the crown
    Of Dreaming-town;
    The sand-man is his name.

    When sparkling eyes
    Troop sleepywise
    And busy lips grow dumb;
    When little heads
    Nod toward the beds,
    We know the sand-man's come.
meaning:
Paul Laurence Dunbar's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Sand-Man"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
